what: Detection and Localization of Executable Files
title: Detection and Localization of Executable Files
text: package helps locate external programs “python”, “Google Chrome”, “node.js” “LibreOffice” can called easily R. makes easier call programs R across various operating systems.

title: Detection and Localization of Executable Files
text: motivated need tools similar rmarkdown::pandoc_available() rmarkdown::pandoc_exec() allowing locate external programs pandoc. needed call programs R without pulling heavy dependencies.

title: Detection and Localization of Executable Files
text: can install CRAN version :

title: Detection and Localization of Executable Files
text: Package pagedown provides find_chrome() finding “Google Chrome”. Package findpython provides find_python_cmd() finding “python”. want go deeper ‘R’ ‘node.js’, recommend package packer. want go deeper ‘R’ ‘Python’, recommend package reticulate.

what: Check executable availability and version — exec_available
title: Check executable availability and version — exec_available
text: determine whether executable currently available user machine (optionally checking specific greater version).

title: Check executable availability and version — exec_available
Check executable availability and version — exec_available
text: exec executable identifier, single character. Use one values: chrome: 'Google Chrome' executable firefox: 'Mozilla Firefox' executable libreoffice: 'LibreOffice' executable node: 'node.js' executable npm: 'npm' executable air: 'air' R formatter language server executable pandoc: 'Pandoc' document converter executable python: 'python' executable pip: 'pip' executable excel: 'Microsoft Excel' executable word: 'Microsoft Word' executable powerpoint: 'Microsoft PowerPoint' executable version required version executable error TRUE, executes error action executable required version found.

title: Check executable availability and version — exec_available
text: logical indicating whether executable available.

title: Check executable availability and version — exec_available
code:
if (exec_available("python")) {   cat("python", as.character(exec_version("python")), "is available!
") } #> python 3.12.3 is available! if(exec_available("python", "3.9")) {   cat("required version of python is available!
") } #> required version of python is available! if (exec_available("pip")) {   cat("pip", as.character(exec_version("pip")), "is available!
") } #> pip 24.0 is available! if (exec_available("pip", "19.3")) {   cat("pip", as.character(exec_version("pip")), "is available!
") } #> pip 24.0 is available! if (exec_available("chrome")) {   cat("chrome", as.character(exec_version("chrome")), "is available!
") } if (exec_available("firefox")) {   cat("firefox", as.character(exec_version("firefox")), "is available!
") } #> firefox 150.0.3 is available! if (exec_available("node")) {   cat("node.js", as.character(exec_version("node")), "is available!
") } #> node.js 22.22.3 is available! if (exec_available("npm")) {   cat("npm", as.character(exec_version("npm")), "is available!
") } #> npm 10.9.8 is available! if (exec_available("npm", version = "10.13.0")) {   cat("npm", as.character(exec_version("npm")), "is available!
") } if (exec_available("air")) {   cat("air", as.character(exec_version("air")), "is available!
") } if (exec_available("pandoc")) {   cat("pandoc", as.character(exec_version("pandoc")), "is available!
") } #> pandoc 3.8.3 is available! if (exec_available("excel")) {   cat("excel", as.character(exec_version("excel")), "is available!
") } if (exec_available("word")) {   cat("word", as.character(exec_version("word")), "is available!
") } if (exec_available("powerpoint")) {   cat("powerpoint", as.character(exec_version("powerpoint")), "is available!
") }

title: Find an executable — exec_locate
text: Searches executable places use highest version found (unless specific version requested). function mimic behavior rmarkdown::find_pandoc() function order locate programs. methods work differently depending OS program: Windows, search 'Google Chrome', 'Mozilla Firefox', 'Microsoft Word', 'Microsoft PowerPoint' 'Microsoft Excel' done registry, means one version can chosen, one referenced registry. (still can force another path argument dir). 'Microsoft Word', 'Microsoft PowerPoint' 'Microsoft Excel' can located linux OS. search 'pip' using result search 'python' find corresponding 'pip' executable. search 'pandoc' also looks directory referenced RSTUDIO_PANDOC environment variable (copy bundled 'RStudio' 'Quarto'), addition PATH.

title: Find an executable — exec_locate
code: exec_locate(exec, cache = TRUE, dir = NULL, version = NULL)

title: Find an executable — exec_locate
text: exec executable identifier, single character. Use one values: chrome: 'Google Chrome' executable firefox: 'Mozilla Firefox' executable libreoffice: 'LibreOffice' executable node: 'node.js' executable npm: 'npm' executable air: 'air' R formatter language server executable pandoc: 'Pandoc' document converter executable python: 'python' executable pip: 'pip' executable excel: 'Microsoft Excel' executable word: 'Microsoft Word' executable powerpoint: 'Microsoft PowerPoint' executable cache FALSE, search executable even executable found previously. dir character vector directory paths executable may found. version version executable look (e.g., "14.15.4"). NULL (default), searches highest version.

title: Find an executable — exec_locate
text: list containing path executable version found. found, version 0 exec_file NULL.

title: Find an executable — exec_locate
text: Ubuntu platforms, 'LibreOffice' require add environment variable LD_LIBRARY_PATH following path: /usr/lib/libreoffice/program (see message "libreglo.open shared object file" case). can done R command Sys.setenv(LD_LIBRARY_PATH = "/usr/lib/libreoffice/program/")

title: Find an executable — exec_locate
code: exec_locate("firefox") #> $exec_file #> [1] "/usr/bin/firefox" #>  #> $version #> [1] ‘150.0.3’ #>  exec_locate("chrome") #> $exec_file #> NULL #>  #> $version #> [1] ‘0’ #>  exec_locate("chrome", version = "88.0.4324.150") #> $exec_file #> NULL #>  #> $version #> [1] ‘0’ #>  exec_locate("libreoffice") #> $exec_file #> NULL #>  #> $version #> [1] ‘0’ #>  exec_locate("node") #> $exec_file #> [1] "/usr/local/bin/node" #>  #> $version #> [1] ‘22.22.3’ #>  exec_locate("npm") #> $exec_file #> [1] "/usr/local/bin/npm" #>  #> $version #> [1] ‘10.9.8’ #>  exec_locate("air") #> $exec_file #> NULL #>  #> $version #> [1] ‘0’ #>  exec_locate("pandoc") #> $exec_file #> [1] "/opt/hostedtoolcache/pandoc/3.8.3/x64/pandoc" #>  #> $version #> [1] ‘3.8.3’ #>  exec_locate("python") #> $exec_file #> [1] "/usr/bin/python3" #>  #> $version #> [1] ‘3.12.3’ #>  exec_locate("pip") #> $exec_file #> [1] "/usr/bin/pip3" #>  #> $version #> [1] ‘24.0’ #>  exec_locate("excel") #> $exec_file #> NULL #>  #> $version #> [1] ‘0’ #>  exec_locate("word") #> $exec_file #> NULL #>  #> $version #> [1] ‘0’ #>  exec_locate("powerpoint") #> $exec_file #> NULL #>  #> $version #> [1] ‘0’ #>

title: Get executable version — exec_version
text: get version executable.

title: Get executable version — exec_version
text: exec executable identifier, single character. Use one values: chrome: 'Google Chrome' executable firefox: 'Mozilla Firefox' executable libreoffice: 'LibreOffice' executable node: 'node.js' executable npm: 'npm' executable air: 'air' R formatter language server executable pandoc: 'Pandoc' document converter executable python: 'python' executable pip: 'pip' executable excel: 'Microsoft Excel' executable word: 'Microsoft Word' executable powerpoint: 'Microsoft PowerPoint' executable

title: Get executable version — exec_version
text: numeric_version() version executable found.

title: Get executable version — exec_version
code: if(exec_available("chrome"))   message(exec_version("chrome")) if(exec_available("node"))   message(exec_version("node")) #> 22.22.3 if(exec_available("air"))   message(exec_version("air")) if(exec_available("pandoc"))   message(exec_version("pandoc")) #> 3.8.3 if(exec_available("python"))   message(exec_version("python")) #> 3.12.3 if(exec_available("libreoffice"))   message(exec_version("libreoffice")) if(exec_available("word"))   message(exec_version("word")) if(exec_available("powerpoint"))   message(exec_version("powerpoint")) if(exec_available("firefox"))   message(exec_version("firefox")) #> 150.0.3

title: locatexec 0.1.2
text: Add detection ‘air’ executable (R formatter). Add detection ‘Pandoc’ executable. Make version parsing robust: executable emitting unexpected --version output (e.g. Firefox snap wrapper Ubuntu) longer raises “invalid version specification” reported found. Fix min_version_available(version=...) Set NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 calling node/npm avoid leaving node-compile-cache directory temp directory.

title: locatexec 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2021-05-19 Added timeout system2 called. change python reference order execs, see .exec_names$python .exec_names$pip
